steps.content: Apply the triangle inequality theorem for all three pairs of sides. Let the known sides be 7 and 12, and the unknown side be $x$. The three conditions are: (1) $7 + 12 \gt x$, (2) $7 + x \gt 12$, and (3) $12 + x \gt 7$.
Simplify each inequality. From (1): $x \lt 19$. From (2): $x \gt 5$. From (3): $x \gt -5$, which is always true for any positive length.
Combine the binding constraints: $x \gt 5$ and $x \lt 19$, giving $5 \lt x \lt 19$.

• "$x \lt 5$": Allows only sides shorter than 5, but then $7 + x \lt 12$ violates the inequality. Eliminated.
  • "$x \gt 19$": Allows only sides longer than 19, but then $7 + 12 = 19$ is not greater than $x$. Eliminated.
  • "$5 \lt x \lt 19$": Satisfies all three triangle inequality conditions simultaneously. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
  • "$x \lt 5$ or $x \gt 19$": This is the union of the two eliminated ranges; neither region forms a valid triangle. Eliminated.
